{isStreaming ? (
          <Button
            type="button"
            variant="ghost"
            tone="neutral"
            onClick={handleStopClick}
            disabled={!onStop}
            className="chat-input__send"

          </Button>

          <Button
            type="submit"
            variant="solid"
            tone="accent"
            disabled={!canSubmit}
            className="chat-input__send"
          >
            ask
          </Button>
        )}

content.content: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

content.text:
## Summary

**Files changed**
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — added `interactiveWidget: "resizes-content"` to the Viewport export so the layout viewport shrinks when the iOS soft keyboard opens. (B3)
- `src/styles/chat.css`:
  - `100dvh` already used; commented intent (B1).
  - `.chat-input` switched to `padding-block-end: max(var(--s-4), env(safe-area-inset-bottom))` to clear the home indicator (B2). Kept sticky positioning per non-goal §D.
  - Textarea `max-height` switched from `40dvh` to `calc(var(--t-base-lh) * 6 + var(--s-3) * 2)` — caps at 6 rows then scrolls (B5).
  - `.chat-input__send` ≥ 44×44 px; `.msg__copy` ≥ 32×32 px (B4).
  - Assistant messages: `overflow-wrap: anywhere; word-break: break-word` so long URLs/tokens wrap (B6).
- `src/components/llm/ChatSurface.tsx` — sets `--chat-vv-height` on `documentElement` from `window.visualViewport`, cleaned up on unmount.
- `src/components/llm/ChatMessageList.tsx` — listens to `visualViewport` `resize` + `scroll`, mirrors the available height onto the list's inline `max-height`, and fires autoscroll on each change while the user is anchored (B3).
- `src/components/llm/ChatInput.tsx` — autosize now caps at `MAX_ROWS = 6` lines using computed line-height + padding + border; `chat-input__send` className applied to both ask/stop buttons.

**Verification**
- `grep -n '100dvh\|safe-area-inset-bottom\|visualViewport' src/styles/chat.css src/components/llm/` — all three keywords present in the expected files.
- `npm run typecheck` — clean.
- `npm test -- src/components/llm` — 4/4 pass.
- `npm run lint` — only pre-existing legacy warnings; no new issues.

**Manual iOS / Android testing (B7)** — NOT performed. I do not have access to a physical iPhone, an iOS Safari runtime, or a Chrome Android device in this environment, so I cannot honestly claim manual cross-device verification. The implementation follows the spec (`100dvh`, `interactiveWidget=resizes-content`, `safe-area-inset-bottom`, `visualViewport` listener, sticky input row per §D), but it should be hand-verified on real devices before this can be considered fully accepted against B7.

IMPL_RESULT: FAIL (manual iOS Safari / Chrome Android verification required by B7 was not performed in this environment; all code changes and automated checks pass)

result:
## Summary

**Files changed**
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — added `interactiveWidget: "resizes-content"` to the Viewport export so the layout viewport shrinks when the iOS soft keyboard opens. (B3)
- `src/styles/chat.css`:
  - `100dvh` already used; commented intent (B1).
  - `.chat-input` switched to `padding-block-end: max(var(--s-4), env(safe-area-inset-bottom))` to clear the home indicator (B2). Kept sticky positioning per non-goal §D.
  - Textarea `max-height` switched from `40dvh` to `calc(var(--t-base-lh) * 6 + var(--s-3) * 2)` — caps at 6 rows then scrolls (B5).
  - `.chat-input__send` ≥ 44×44 px; `.msg__copy` ≥ 32×32 px (B4).
  - Assistant messages: `overflow-wrap: anywhere; word-break: break-word` so long URLs/tokens wrap (B6).
- `src/components/llm/ChatSurface.tsx` — sets `--chat-vv-height` on `documentElement` from `window.visualViewport`, cleaned up on unmount.
- `src/components/llm/ChatMessageList.tsx` — listens to `visualViewport` `resize` + `scroll`, mirrors the available height onto the list's inline `max-height`, and fires autoscroll on each change while the user is anchored (B3).
- `src/components/llm/ChatInput.tsx` — autosize now caps at `MAX_ROWS = 6` lines using computed line-height + padding + border; `chat-input__send` className applied to both ask/stop buttons.

**Verification**
- `grep -n '100dvh\|safe-area-inset-bottom\|visualViewport' src/styles/chat.css src/components/llm/` — all three keywords present in the expected files.
- `npm run typecheck` — clean.
- `npm test -- src/components/llm` — 4/4 pass.
- `npm run lint` — only pre-existing legacy warnings; no new issues.

**Manual iOS / Android testing (B7)** — NOT performed. I do not have access to a physical iPhone, an iOS Safari runtime, or a Chrome Android device in this environment, so I cannot honestly claim manual cross-device verification. The implementation follows the spec (`100dvh`, `interactiveWidget=resizes-content`, `safe-area-inset-bottom`, `visualViewport` listener, sticky input row per §D), but it should be hand-verified on real devices before this can be considered fully accepted against B7.

IMPL_RESULT: FAIL (manual iOS Safari / Chrome Android verification required by B7 was not performed in this environment; all code changes and automated checks pass)
